metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.finding: Child ticket a37b-f1d3-ae2d-44b1 (Enable progressive drift-refresh by default: drift-only-stale re-reviews run the full pipeline) appears in the session log's ticket graph as a child of this epic but is entirely absent from the plan's Context, Success Criteria, Acceptance Criteria, and Definitions sections — no rationale is given for its omission.

Child ticket a8e5-6876-dcde-4d3d (Pass-2 grounding hardening + finding hygiene: absence-claim veto, DET content filtering) appears in the session log's ticket graph as a child of this epic but is entirely absent from the plan's Context, Success Criteria, Acceptance Criteria, and Definitions sections — no rationale is given for its omission.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen AC2(b) to state an outcome the artifact must enable (e.g., 'running the listed command against the `REVIEW_RESULT` store produces a numeric block-count and a false-block-count that can be compared to the baseline'), so completeness is evaluable by outcome not by presence.
findings.finding: AC2 is framed as an activity ('record an artifact', 'write queries', 'add a link') rather than as an observable outcome, making it difficult to verify the artifact is correct or useful rather than merely present.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (the in-session measurement-framework artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the current AC2 framing with a criterion of the form: 'A measurement-framework comment exists on this epic containing: (a) the baseline numbers, (b) CLI/query invocations that, when run against the production `REVIEW_RESULT` (code_review_result_v1) sidecar store, emit block-count and false-block-count figures, and advisory `norm_id` survival rates — verified to run without error on a staging or production replica, and (c) a link to ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate an owner (or the epic itself) to produce a single epic-comment artifact covering: (a) the 0/162 baseline + ~13 advisories/round, (b) re-runnable queries for BOTH code-review block counts AND advisory-uptake norm_id survival, and (c) the link to deferred ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d — at merge time, before epic close.
findings.finding: AC2 of the parent — the in-session measurement-framework comment on the epic containing (a) the pre-change baseline, (b) re-runnable queries for BOTH code-review block/false-block counts AND advisory-uptake norm_id survival, and (c) a link to the deferred follow-up ticket — is only partially covered. e1f2 commits to recording the advisory-uptake observation plan on the epic, but no child owns the code-review block/false-block baseline and re-runnable queries or the link to 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d as a deliverable on the epic itself.
findings.location: child e1f2-0bcc-dbe7-4750 (advisory coverage); gap spans all children (no owner for code-review measurement portion or follow-up link)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC to the epic (or assign to e1f2/b9c0 as a joint deliverable) requiring the consolidated epic comment. e1f2 is the most natural owner since it already commits to recording on the epic — extend its scope to include the code-review block baseline and the follow-up link.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add epic-level ACs (or explicit AC sub-bullets) that independently verify: (a) the ADR-0036 A/B gate result is recorded before code-blocking is enabled, (b) completion-verifier FAIL persistence and all-unmet-criteria enumeration are confirmed in a test or log artifact, and (c) the signed-children anti-pattern documentation exists at a specified location.
findings.finding: Three Success Criteria contain specific verifiable sub-properties — (SC1) 'passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping', (SC3) 'FAILs are durably persisted and enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict; the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented', (SC4) 'fire-rate observable from day one' — that have no direct epic-level AC. AC1 ('all child tickets are closed') is the sole rollup, meaning no independent AC gates whether the A/B gate was actually passed, whether durable persistence was confirmed, or whether the anti-pattern was documented before closing the epic.
findings.location: Success Criteria section + Acceptance Criteria section (AC item 1)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-deployment rollback path (e.g., a feature flag or fast-disable config) for the serious-tier blocking threshold to the epic or b9c0's ACs, per T4's requirement that an explicit back-out mechanism — not only a pre-deployment gate — be specified.
findings.finding: The blocking-threshold enablement (child b9c0) is a material behavioral change — findings that previously passed as advisories will now BLOCK merges — but the plan names only a pre-deployment A/B gate ('passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping') and provides no post-deployment rollback path (kill-switch flag, feature-flag revert, or fast-disable procedure). Per T4, acknowledgement plus a pre-deploy gate is insufficient; an explicit back-out mechanism is required.
findings.location: Success Criteria (first bullet) and Definitions & Sequencing ('b9c0 enables a block threshold')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to b9c0 such as: 'A documented kill-switch exists (e.g., env var or config flag) that disables serious-tier blocking and degrades gracefully to advisory, with no code change required; the flag's location and revert procedure are documented in CLAUDE.md or the operator runbook.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate in the epic (or the relevant child ticket's ACs) exactly which CLAUDE.md sections and any linked docs currently describing mandatory advisory remediation must be updated, so no stale references remain after the change lands.
findings.finding: The plan states 'agent guidance no longer MANDATES advisory remediation' (a change to CLAUDE.md), but does not enumerate which specific CLAUDE.md sections, references, or downstream docs currently describe the mandatory-remediation rule — leaving existing references to the mandate potentially stale after the change lands.
findings.location: Success Criteria (second bullet): 'Agent guidance no longer MANDATES advisory remediation'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a doc-cleanup AC such as: 'All CLAUDE.md sections and referenced runbooks that describe mandatory advisory remediation are updated to reflect judgment-based guidance; a grep/search for the old mandate language is run and all hits are resolved before closing this ticket.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the derived block-threshold value is stored (e.g., a named constant in a config module, a YAML/TOML config file, or a feature-flag entry) so future re-derivations are config changes, not code changes, and the test fixture points to the same authoritative source.
findings.finding: The block threshold derived for code-v2 serious-tier criteria is described as 'pinned in a test' (via b9c0), but the plan does not specify whether the threshold value will be stored in a configurable/externalizable location or hardcoded into the codebase. Since the whole epic's premise is periodic recalibration and the plan explicitly defers a new threshold derivation for code-v3, this value is certain to evolve — hardcoding it makes future re-derivation a code change rather than a config update, and risks the calibrated value being silently stale.
findings.location: Definitions & Sequencing — 'child b9c0 enumerates the exact enabled set and pins it in a test'; Success Criteria bullet 1
findings.suggested_fix: Define the serious-tier threshold (e.g., 0.9 crossover value and the enabled-criterion set) as named constants in a single config module (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/thresholds.py`) imported by both `decide.py` and the b9c0 test fixture. When code-v3 thresholds are derived, a new entry is added to the same module without touching decision logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a child ticket (or explicitly assign SC3 to an existing child) that specifies the mechanism for durably persisting completion-verifier FAIL verdicts, emitting all unmet criteria in a single verdict, and documenting the signed-children AC anti-pattern — and ensure the epic's close-path AC covers it.
findings.finding: Success Criterion 3 ('Completion-verifier FAILs are durably persisted and enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict; the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented') is stated as an epic deliverable, but no child ticket covers this work. The four named children (9f25, b9c0, f32e, d4cf) address code-review blocking and G7 only. Bug 02a3 is linked relates_to the epic (not as a child) and its own ACs only characterize the problem; it proposes no fix. The epic's close-path AC ('all child tickets are closed') will not verify SC3 if SC3 has no delivery vehicle. The plan names the problem and the goal but leaves the mechanism — what changes, in which files, in which child ticket — entirely unspecified.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a child ticket scoped to: (a) a completion-verdict sidecar emit (analogous to code_review/sidecar.py) that persists FAIL verdicts to the ticket store, (b) a reconcile/verifier change ensuring a single pass surfaces all unmet criteria rather than stopping at the first, and (c) documentation (CLAUDE.md or docs/plan-review-gate.md) of the signed-children AC anti-pattern and the certifiable:false escape hatch. Link it as a child of this epic and add it to the AC coverage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign one child (or the epic itself at merge time) to file ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d in idea state, linked discovered_from this epic, with [operator-attested] on the data-collection criterion, before epic close.
findings.finding: AC3 of the parent — the deferred post-deployment measurement filed as a separate follow-up ticket with id 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d, linked discovered_from this epic, parked in idea, data-collection criterion [operator-attested] — is not covered by any child. No sibling mentions creating or verifying this ticket's existence.
findings.location: No child — AC3 is absent from the entire sibling roster
findings.suggested_fix: Add a thin child ticket or extend e1f2 (the natural measurement-framework owner) to include: create/verify ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d in idea with the correct link and [operator-attested] tag.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten b9c0's dependency on 9f25 by explicitly requiring that b9c0 not be claimed until 9f25's threshold comment is recorded, and that b9c0's criteria_routing.json entry use the exact value from 9f25 — not the '~0.6' approximation.
findings.finding: b9c0 specifies the block_threshold as '~0.6' derived from 9f25, but its Acceptance Criteria do not name the threshold as a TBD-from-9f25 value — they state it as a near-concrete '~0.6'. If b9c0 is claimed before 9f25 completes (or if 9f25's crossover lands at 0.54 or 0.5), b9c0's criteria_routing.json value and its pinning test will encode a threshold not justified by the derivation, making the b9c0 AC 'A replay of the post-recal corpus shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK' potentially false at 0.6 if the actual crossover is lower.
findings.location: child b9c0-2b03-36de-469f — What section and Acceptance Criteria; dependency on child 9f25-ca33-74de-40bf
findings.suggested_fix: In b9c0's What section, replace '~0.6' with 'the value recorded in 9f25's threshold comment (TBD; b9c0 is blocked on 9f25 closing)' and add a claim-gating note that b9c0 must not be implemented until 9f25's crossover analysis is complete.

findings.finding: Parent AC-2 — the in-session measurement-framework artifact (a) pre-change baseline recorded as epic comment, (b) exact re-runnable queries/commands for code-review block/false-block counts AND plan-review advisory-uptake, (c) link to the deferred follow-up ticket — has no child that owns producing this composite artifact. e1f2 partially mentions recording an observation plan, and 9f25 produces adjudication artifacts, but no child is scoped to assemble and record the three-part AC-2 artifact as a comment on the epic.
findings.location: Parent Acceptance Criteria — AC-2; no single child
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line to one child (e.g., e1f2 or b9c0, whichever closes last) requiring it to post the three-part measurement-framework comment on the epic, or document this as an operator-executed step in the epic's own ACs with [operator-attested] so the completion verifier can pass on attestation.

findings.finding: Parent AC-3 — the deferred post-deployment measurement follow-up ticket (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d) must be filed, linked discovered_from this epic, and parked in idea with a data-collection criterion marked [operator-attested] — is owned by no child in the sibling roster.
findings.location: Parent Acceptance Criteria — AC-3; no child in roster
findings.suggested_fix: Either add an AC line to one child (e.g., the last-to-close child) asserting it confirms the follow-up ticket exists with the correct discovered_from link and idea status, or add [operator-attested] to AC-3 in the parent so the completion verifier can pass on operator attestation rather than requiring a child artifact.

findings.finding: AC3 identifies the follow-up ticket by a UUID-like ID (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d) but does not state whether this ticket already exists (in which case the executor verifies and links it) or must be created (in which case the system may auto-assign a different ID, invalidating the reference). An executor cannot determine which action to take without asking.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'filed as a separate follow-up ticket (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d, linked discovered_from this epic, parked in idea)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d)' with either a direct URL to the pre-existing ticket, or change the AC to read: 'filed as a separate follow-up ticket (record system-assigned ID here at merge time), linked discovered_from this epic, parked in idea.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either number the Success Criteria bullets (so 'Success Criterion 1' resolves) or change the parenthetical in Definitions to a descriptive reference such as '(see Success Criteria: Code-review blocking)'.
findings.finding: The Definitions section labels the serious-tier concept as 'Success Criterion 1' (parenthetical), but the Success Criteria section uses unnumbered bullet points — there is no 'Success Criterion 1' to reference. Any reader trying to locate 'Success Criterion 1' in the Success Criteria section will find only an unlabeled bullet list.
findings.location: Definitions & Sequencing section, first bullet: '**"serious-tier" (Success Criterion 1):**'
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(Success Criterion 1)' to '(see Success Criteria: Code-review blocking)' in the Definitions section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the epic title (or add a subtitle) to reflect the fourth deliverable — e.g., '… + leaf-plan parent-containment criterion (G7/d4cf)' — so the title faithfully covers all four goals.
findings.finding: The plan's title names three goals ('code-review blocking lane + advisory-guidance loosening + completion-verifier fixes') but the body delivers a fourth material goal — a new plan-review criterion (G7) for leaf-plan/parent-plan scope containment (child d4cf). The title omits this entirely, understating the epic's scope.
findings.location: Title / Success Criteria (4th bullet) / Context ('This 4th finding motivates child d4cf')
findings.suggested_fix: Rename to: 'Review-gate calibration 2026-07-12: code-review blocking lane + advisory-guidance loosening + completion-verifier fixes + leaf-plan parent-containment criterion (G7)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Coordinate a8e5 and d4cf to sequence their MOVE_REGISTRY additions (or land one before the other is claimed) and add an integration test asserting BOTH moves are present after both children land.
findings.finding: Both a8e5 and d4cf add entries to the shared coach MOVE_REGISTRY without coordinating their changes. If they land in either order without awareness of the other's addition, one child's merge could overwrite or conflict with the other's registry entry.
findings.location: child a8e5-6876-dcde-4d3d and child d4cf-0780-cbed-4879 — MOVE_REGISTRY
findings.suggested_fix: Have whichever child lands second include a test asserting both its own move and the sibling's move are present in MOVE_REGISTRY.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] If ac0e implements the optional DET lint, sequence it after a8e5 lands so the lint is authored against a8e5's refactored det_floor.py, and ensure the lint includes subject content that survives a8e5's DET hygiene backstop.
findings.finding: a8e5 explicitly modifies det_floor.py (DET content floor for P5/P6 class emitters), and ac0e optionally proposes adding a DET lint to det_floor.py for 'signed' inside epic AC checklist lines. If ac0e implements the optional lint, both children write to det_floor.py without coordination; ac0e's implementer working from a pre-a8e5 baseline could conflict with a8e5's structural changes to the same file.
findings.location: child a8e5-6876-dcde-4d3d and child ac0e-dbfd-eeb7-4b53 — det_floor.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in ac0e that the optional DET lint, if pursued, must be implemented after a8e5 lands and must emit the container ticket id and the offending AC line as evidence/location to pass a8e5's subject-completeness check.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
